title: boerhavia fistulosa
intro: Boerhavia fistulosa, also known as the desert broom or desert rope, is a perennial succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. It is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating kidney stones, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Boerhavia fistulosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for skin conditions and wounds.
